title: Star Wars: The Action Figure Archive

'''''Star Wars: The Action Figure Archive''''' is a reference book first published by [[Neko Publishing]] in Japan in [[December]] [[1997]],<ref name="Cargo Bay Japan" /> written by [[Eimei Takeda]] and [[Seiji Takahashi]]. Covering all [[Hasbro]] and [[Kenner Products]] action figures from [[1977]] to [[1997]] (with a [[1998]] update in later editions), it has over 2000 high-definition photos.<ref name="UK Edition Archive.org" />

[[Stephen J. Sansweet]] and [[Josh Ling]] assisted in the creation of the original book, which Neko described as a "mook"—a hybrid book and magazine. Takeda and a representative from Neko contacted [[Lucasfilm]], which collaborated in the project, with Allan Kausch from Lucasfilm contributing as an editor. Photo assistant Cara Evangelista was also involved; one photographer in Los Angeles and three photographers in Tokyo produced the images for the book with the help of collectors in the United States and the Lucasfilm photo archive.<ref name="UK Edition Archive.org" />

Sansweet and Ling also adapted the book into the English language, with the American edition published in [[1999]] by [[Chronicle Books]]. It was coordinated by Lucy Autrey Wilson and Sarah Malarkey, Julia Bragg, and Mikyla Bruder from Chronicle Books and was updated with some newer figures with the help of Linda Baker in public relations of Hasbro/Kenner. A hardcover version was printed in Hong Kong and released in the UK in the same year by [[Virgin Publishing]].<ref name="UK Edition Archive.org" />
